title: Ensure that the config file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the config file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that the config file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: The config file controls various parameters that set the behavior of various components of the master node. You should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be writable by only the administrators on the system.
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the master node. For example, stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/config Verify that the permissions are 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the master node. For example, chmod 644 /etc/kubernetes/config Impact: None
input_row.default_value: By default, config file has permissions of 644 .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control specifies checking file permissions on the config file, which is typically a host-level check. However, since the control is related to Kubernetes configuration, it can be inferred that the Kubernetes API or kubectl may expose relevant information about the configuration and its permissions.
- The audit mentions using a command to check file permissions, which implies that there may be a way to verify this through Kubernetes configurations or API calls.
- The rationale emphasizes maintaining the integrity of the config file, which aligns with Kubernetes security best practices.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_config_file_permissions_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use `kubectl` to check the configuration of the Kubernetes components that may reference the config file.
- The command `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].volumeMounts[?(@.mountPath=="/etc/kubernetes")].name}'` can be used to identify if any pods are mounting the config file directory.
- Additionally, check the deployment or stateful set configurations that may reference the config file.
- Validate that the permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive by checking the relevant configurations in the Kubernetes API.
- Pass/Fail criteria: The expected value for the config file permissions should be 644 or more restrictive. If the permissions are less than 644, it would be a fail condition.
